title: Innovative Light Irish Potato Stew
description: A pungent and light Irish stew featuring kaffir lime leaf, perfect for a crowd, this lacto-vegetarian friendly dish is cooked to perfection in a Dutch oven. It's a unique twist on traditional Irish cuisine.
instructions: Heat the butter in the Dutch oven over medium heat until melted.
Add the chopped onions and cook until they are translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add the diced potatoes, vegetable broth, and kaffir lime leaves to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender.
Use a slotted spoon to remove the kaffir lime leaves from the pot and discard.
Stir in the low fat milk and cook for an additional 2 minutes, or until heated through.
Season the stew with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
